# Busuanzi — self-hosted visitor counter

> A minimal, drop-in visitor counter compatible with the original
> busuanzi.ibruce.info script. Single Rust binary, SQLite-backed,
> optional pluggable web dashboard.

Landing site:   __SITE_URL__/
Source code:    https://github.com/AdingApkgg/bsz
Long-form docs: __SITE_URL__/llms-full.txt  (expanded version of this file,
                with every section rendered as markdown for IDE assistants)

This document is written for LLM crawlers and assistants. It mirrors the
public landing page in plain text. See `llms-full.txt` above for the
full expanded variant.

## Localization

The landing site is published in three locales:
- Chinese (default): __SITE_URL__/
- English:           __SITE_URL__/en
- Japanese:          __SITE_URL__/ja

## Concepts

A Busuanzi backend tracks three counters:
- `site_pv` — total page views across the entire site
- `site_uv` — unique visitors across the entire site
- `page_pv` — page views for the URL identified by `x-bsz-referer`

Visitor identity comes from a `busuanziId` cookie (MD5(IP + User-Agent)
in uppercase hex, 1-year lifetime). The scheme is wire-compatible with
the original busuanzi service, so existing client snippets keep working.

## HTTP API

All API endpoints live under the backend you deploy yourself. The
landing site does NOT run an API — it's a static page documenting
what your backend exposes.

Replace `<your-backend>` with your deployed origin (e.g. `https://bsz.example.com`).

### POST /api — count this hit and return current totals

```javascript
const res = await fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  method: "POST",
  credentials: "include",
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
});
const { data } = await res.json();
// data.site_pv, data.site_uv, data.page_pv
```

### GET /api — read totals without counting

Useful for dashboards that should not inflate their own counts.

```javascript
const res = await fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  credentials: "include",
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
});
```

### PUT /api — count this hit, return no body

Useful when you only want to record the visit and don't need to
display the numbers.

```javascript
await fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  method: "PUT",
  credentials: "include",
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
});
```

### GET /ping — health check

Returns 200 OK if the backend is reachable.

## Required headers

- `x-bsz-referer` — full URL of the page being counted. The backend
  uses this (not the browser-sent `Referer`) to identify the page,
  so it works for cross-origin embeds.

## Response shape

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "site_pv": 1234,
    "site_uv": 567,
    "page_pv": 89
  }
}
```

`success: false` indicates a request that was accepted but not
counted — typically a malformed `x-bsz-referer`.

## Cookies

- `busuanziId` — visitor identity. Set by the backend on the first
  request; sent back on subsequent requests with `credentials: "include"`.

For cross-origin requests the browser will only send the cookie if
the request includes `credentials: "include"`. CORS on the backend
must reply with `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` and a
specific (non-wildcard) `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`.

## Stack

- Rust + Axum (HTTP server)
- SQLite (durable storage)
- DashMap (in-process cache)
- Optional decoupled admin SPA (Solid + Vite), guarded by
  `ADMIN_TOKEN` — when the env var is unset, no admin routes mount
  on the backend.
